summary: Iter-67 testing complete. 7/12 backend pytest pass. CRITICAL BUG FOUND in license-document endpoints: both POST and GET /api/therapists/me/license-document return 422 to every caller because routes/therapists.py:484 and :544 use Depends(require_session) without invoking the factory — FastAPI then exposes the inner function's parameters (allowed_roles, payload) as required body fields. The frontend uploader is wired correctly but no upload can ever succeed against this backend. SMS A2P panel verified live (verdict=blocked_a2p_10dlc with err 30034 banner, A2P PUT/GET roundtrip persists, save toast shows). Research-enrichment scoring fix verified (no-overlap capped at 1.0; primary_hit + deep ≥ 7.0). Matching tiebreaker + differentiator bonus verified (review_signal sorts higher first; differentiator key present in breakdown; top-15 scores have ≥8 unique values across 124 therapists). All Providers action column verified vertical (flex-col items-end gap-1.5, 96×82px, row=116px).

critical.issue: Dependency factory not invoked. Code: `Depends(require_session)` should be `Depends(require_session(("therapist",)))`. Currently every request returns HTTP 422 with body `{detail:[{loc:[body,allowed_roles],msg:Field required}, {loc:[body,payload],msg:Field required}]}` because FastAPI introspects require_session's signature (allowed_roles, payload) and treats them as request body fields. The handlers never run. Both POST upload and GET metadata are completely non-functional. Spec says POST should return 200, reject unsupported types with 400, reject >5MB with 400, reject bad base64 with 400, and patient session should get 403 — none of these are reachable.
critical.fix: In /app/backend/routes/therapists.py change `role_email: tuple[str, str] = Depends(require_session)` to `session: dict = Depends(require_session(("therapist",)))` on lines 484 and 544. Then unpack `email = session["email"]` instead of `role, email = role_email` (drop the `if role != "therapist"` check since the factory already enforces it, OR keep the factory parameter as `("therapist",)` for clarity). Note: require_session returns the JWT payload dict, not a (role,email) tuple — the existing tuple-unpacking code is also wrong and needs to read `session.get("email")` and `session.get("role")`.

minor.issue: Top scores still show ties at the rounded-to-1-decimal display level (e.g. 88.8 appears 3×, 84.2 appears 4×, 84.0 appears 3× across the 124-therapist Idaho set when scoring an anxiety request). The differentiator bonus (cap 1.5) successfully diverges the underlying floats but rounding via `round(min(100.0, sum(breakdown.values())), 1)` collapses many of them. Spec says NO two therapists should share the same score — consider rounding to 2 decimals OR using a wider differentiator range OR storing both the rounded display score and the raw score for sorting.

ui_bugs.issue: UI exists with all expected testids (license-doc-uploader, license-doc-input, license-doc-upload-btn, license-doc-current) but the backend endpoint it POSTs to returns 422 — uploads will always fail with toast `Field required: body.allowed_roles`. The bug is in the backend, not the frontend, but it makes the entire feature non-functional end-to-end.

integration_issues.issue: Backend dependency bug breaks the whole flow (see backend critical above). E2E flow could not be validated because POST returns 422 before the handler runs.

action_items: FIX (CRITICAL) /app/backend/routes/therapists.py:484 and :544 — change `Depends(require_session)` to `Depends(require_session(("therapist",)))`. Update handler body to read role/email from the returned payload dict rather than tuple-unpacking. After fix, re-run TestLicenseUpload (5 tests) — they should all pass.

(MINOR) Tighten matching score divergence — current rounding produces multiple 88.8s and 84.2s within the top 15. Either round to 2 decimals on display OR widen the differentiator bonus range OR sort by raw float and display rounded.
(CARRY-OVER from iter-66) admin.py count=0 clamp bug still open.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/therapists.py:484, :544 — Depends(require_session) is missing the factory invocation. This is a pattern that the same file uses correctly on line 236 with `Depends(require_session(("therapist",)))`. Easy oversight — could be caught by a quick lint that flags Depends(callable_factory) without parens.

routes/therapists.py:484 — type hint says `role_email: tuple[str, str]` but require_session's inner _dep returns a dict (the JWT payload). Even if the factory were invoked correctly the tuple unpacking `role, email = role_email` would fail at runtime. Both bugs need fixing together.

matching.py:397 — `total = round(min(100.0, sum(breakdown.values())), 1)` rounds to 1 decimal which defeats the differentiator's 0-1.5 fractional bonus when raw scores cluster within 0.05 of each other. Round to 2 decimals or store raw and rounded separately.
research_enrichment.py:354-380 — fix is good. The new branching is readable and the 1.0 cap on no-overlap is defensible.
AdminDashboard.jsx is still ~4060+ lines (carry-over). SmsStatusPanel.jsx adding ~280 lines is reasonable and well-scoped.

success_rate.backend: 58% (7/12 — all 5 license-upload failures share the same root cause)
success_rate.frontend: 67% (SMS panel + All Providers vertical icons verified; license uploader UI present but blocked by backend 422)

test_credentials: Admin master pwd admin123! via 'Master password' tab → password input → Enter (Sign in button got blocked by sticky nav popover; pressing Enter inside the password field worked). Therapist token mintable via deps._create_session_token(email, role='therapist'). Patient token via same helper.
seed_data_creation: PUT /api/admin/sms-status/a2p stored brand_id=BN_TEST_67 / campaign_id=CM_TEST_67 / status=brand_pending / notes='iter67 test - delete me' in db.app_config. POST /api/admin/test-sms triggered a Twilio call which stored last_test_sms with error_code 30034 in db.app_config (expected, the +16467606274 number is unregistered).
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes the require_session factory invocation in routes/therapists.py:484 and :544, the 5 license-upload pytest cases in test_iteration67_sms_license_match.py should all pass. The handler also needs to switch from `role, email = role_email` (currently tuple-unpacking a dict) to reading `session.get("email")` from the payload. The test file already uses Depends-correct patient-headers fixture to verify the 403 path. Don't re-trigger /admin/test-sms more than once per minute — it actually calls Twilio. The differentiator-divergence test uses the live therapists collection and asserts at least 8 unique scores in the top 15 (currently 8/15 = passes).
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE for all 5 license-upload failures: routes/therapists.py defines two endpoints with `Depends(require_session)` (line 484 POST, line 544 GET) but require_session is a factory function defined in deps.py:116 as `def require_session(allowed_roles: tuple[str, ...]):` returning an inner async _dep. By passing the factory itself (not the result of calling it), FastAPI introspects require_session's signature and finds one positional parameter `allowed_roles` — which it treats as a required body field. It also somehow surfaces `payload` (probably from the handler's own parameter list shadowing) as required. Net result: every request to either endpoint gets back HTTP 422 with body `{detail:[{loc:[body,allowed_roles]}, {loc:[body,payload]}]}` and the actual handler code never executes. REPRO: `curl -X POST $BASE/api/therapists/me/license-document -H 'Authorization: Bearer <valid_therapist_jwt>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"filename":"a.pdf","content_type":"application/pdf","data_base64":"JVBERi0xLjQK"}'` → HTTP 422. FIX: line 484 → `session: dict = Depends(require_session(("therapist",)))`, then `email = session.get("email")`; same on line 544. Drop the manual `if role != 'therapist'` check (the factory enforces it). MITIGATION: until fixed, no therapist can upload a license document; the pending_reapproval flag will not flip; admin will not be able to re-verify any therapist that uses this UI flow.
